title: potentilla anglica subsp. nesogenes
intro: Potentilla anglica subsp. nesogenes, also known as the lesser cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat wounds, skin irritations, and inflammation due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Potentilla anglica subsp. nesogenes include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its medicinal properties can also be extracted using methods such as maceration and percolation.
